paper_title: Environmental Sample Collection and Molecular Analysis

methods.description: Collection of seawater samples from different oceanographic regions (DCM and UIW layers) during specific research cruises, followed by filtration and storage for subsequent molecular analysis.

steps.description: Sea water samples were collected in the sea ice minimum year 2012 on board RV Polarstern on two cruise legs (ARK-XXVII/2 and 3). The first cruise leg, ARK-XXVII/2 includes one station in the Norwegian Sea and a transect through Fram Strait from 11°58.362′ to 11°5.09′ E longitude at 78°50′ N latitude. The second cruise leg, ARK-XXVII/3 covered the Eastern Arctic Ocean, including sampling in the Barents Sea, and at the edge of the Kara Sea and Laptev Sea and at one high-Arctic sampling station (Fig. 1a).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect seawater samples from specified locations and dates during research cruises.
inputs: Sampling locations (Norwegian Sea, Fram Strait, Eastern Arctic Ocean, Barents Sea, Kara Sea, Laptev Sea, high-Arctic station)

steps.description: The chlorophyll maximum layer was sampled with a CTD rosette (with conductivity, temperature and depth profiler parameters recorded) equipped with an array of 10 L Niskin bottles, while the UIW was sampled with a portable CTD.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample specific water layers (DCM and UIW) using CTD and Niskin bottles.

steps.description: From each depth, subsamples of 2 L were sequential filtered under 200 mbar onto 0.4 µm polycarbonate filters (Whatman)
steps.searchable_summary: Filter 2 L subsamples of seawater onto 0.4 µm polycarbonate filters under pressure.

steps.description: and stored at −80°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Store filtered samples at -80°C.

methods.description: Isolation of DNA from filtered environmental samples using a commercial kit with specific modifications.

steps.description: DNA was isolated with the Power Soil DNA extraction kit (MOBIO) following the manufacturer's instructions
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate DNA from samples using the Power Soil DNA extraction kit.

steps.description: including a 2 × 20 s bead beating step to force the breakup of fungal cell walls
steps.searchable_summary: Perform bead beating step during DNA extraction.

steps.description: and a second elution step to increase total DNA yield.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a second elution step during DNA extraction.

methods.description: Amplification of specific marker gene regions (ITS2 and SSU rDNA V4) from extracted DNA using Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) with specific primers and thermal cycling conditions.

steps.description: To study fungal diversity the ITS2 marker region was amplified with the primer combination ITS3Kyo2 (5′-GATGAAGAACGYAGYRAA-3′) and ITS4 (5′-TCCTCCGCTTATTGATATGC-3′) 54,55 .
steps.searchable_summary: Amplify ITS2 marker region using specified primers.

steps.description: To investigate the branching position of phylogenetically disparate putative chytrid groups and to provide additional data on potential hosts organisms the wider eukaryotic community was sampled using PCR amplification of the SSU rDNA V4 region. The V4 region was amplified with the primer combination D512F (5′-ATTCCAGCTCCAATAGCG-3′) and D978R (5′-GACTACGATGGTATCTAATC-3′) 56 .
steps.searchable_summary: Amplify SSU rDNA V4 region using specified primers.
outputs: Amplified SSU rDNA V4 DNA fragments

steps.description: PCR reactions were carried out in a 25 µl reaction volume in triplicate, including 12.5 µl NEBNext PCR mix (New England BioLabs), 5 µl of 1 µM primer each (forward and reverse) and 4 ng DNA template.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare PCR reactions with specified volumes and concentrations in triplicate.

steps.description: The PCR amplification protocol was the same for both gene targets and consisted of an initial denaturation step at 95°C for 3 min, followed by 32 cycles of denaturation at 95°C for 30 s, annealing at 53°C for 2 min and elongation at 72°C for 30 s and a final elongation at 72°C for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PCR thermal cycling using the specified protocol.

methods.description: High-throughput sequencing of amplified DNA libraries using the Illumina platform.

steps.description: A total of 22 samples were subjected to Illumina sequencing (2 × 300 bp) using the Nano Flow Cell approach.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Illumina sequencing (2x300 bp) on 22 samples using Nano Flow Cell.
outputs: Raw sequence data (2 x 300 bp reads)

steps.description: All generated sequence data have been deposited in NCBI Sequence Read Archive (SRA) under the accession number PRJNA561496.
steps.searchable_summary: Deposit raw sequence data in NCBI SRA.
outputs: Data deposited in NCBI SRA (Accession PRJNA561496)

methods.method_name: Sequence Data Processing and Taxonomic Assignment
methods.description: Processing of raw Illumina sequence data using the DADA2 pipeline in R for quality control, sequence variant identification, and taxonomic classification.

steps.description: Raw sequence data were processed using the DADA2 package in R for sequence variant identification removing the requirement for one universal standard threshold for all cluster groups, i.e., 'taxa' 57 .
steps.searchable_summary: Process raw sequence data using the DADA2 package in R.

steps.description: In brief, sequence processing was carried out as follows: firstly, demultiplexed raw reads were subjected to DADA2 and quality profiles analysed
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze quality profiles of demultiplexed raw reads using DADA2.

steps.description: and secondly, reads were pre-processed by trimming, filtering and de-replication in order to identify unique sequences. Settings for both data sets were the same (maxN = 0, truncQ = 2, maxEE = 2), except for the length parameter (minLen = 250 bp for the V4 and minLen = 50 bp for the ITS2 data set).
steps.searchable_summary: Trim, filter, and de-replicate reads using specified parameters.
inputs: Filtering parameters (maxN=0, truncQ=2, maxEE=2, minLen=250 for V4, minLen=50 for ITS2)

steps.description: Next, after this de-noising process, sequence variants in each sample were inferred
steps.searchable_summary: Infer sequence variants (ASVs/ESVs) after de-noising.

steps.description: and the paired-reads were merged.
steps.searchable_summary: Merge paired-end reads.

steps.description: Finally, chimeras were identified and removed from the read data before taxonomic assignment.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and remove chimeric sequences.

steps.description: Taxonomic classification of the V4 sequences was based on the SILVA reference database version 128 58 .
steps.searchable_summary: Assign taxonomy to V4 sequence variants using the SILVA database.

steps.description: Since reference sequences of the ITS2 region are not as comprehensive as for the V4 region, taxonomic classification was carried out by combining multiple public databases such as: UNITE, NCBI GenBank nt and ITS2 DB V.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign taxonomy to ITS2 sequence variants using multiple databases.
